title: Today's AI Brief: Salesforce and Google's AI Moves
summary: A look at Salesforce's new Slackbot AI agent and Google's Phone app scam detection
imageAlt: Image from source article for Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
imageCaption: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
imageSourceTitle: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. We're looking at some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. First up, Salesforce has rolled out a new version of Slackbot, its workplace assistant. This isn't just a simple notification tool anymore; it's now a fully powered AI agent.

According to VentureBeat, Salesforce has transformed Slackbot to help it battle Microsoft and Google in the workplace AI space. This move is part of a larger platform shift, where companies are trying to create more intelligent and interactive work environments. What stands out to me is how Salesforce is using AI to enhance the user experience, making it more intuitive and helpful for employees.

Also in the news, Google's Phone app is getting a scam detection feature. As reported by The Verge, the app will now flag suspicious calls that might be impersonating one of your contacts. I am looking at this as a great example of AI being used for safety and trust. By using machine learning algorithms, Google's Phone app can help protect users from scams and unwanted calls.

Next, it's worth noting that these developments are part of a larger trend. Companies like Salesforce and Google are investing heavily in AI research and development. My read is that we're going to see more and more AI-powered features in our everyday tools and apps. It's an exciting time for AI, and I'll be keeping an eye on these advancements.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
